Results:

- Native bin check: passed

What’s now implemented in the Bus/Protocol view:
- `/` enters filter mode
- typed characters update the live filter
- Backspace edits filter text
- Enter applies/exits filter mode
- Esc closes filter mode
- `c` clears the filter
- filter state is visible in the Bus pane title:
  - active edit: `[...]_`
  - applied filter: `[...]`

Current validated TUI improvements:
- protocol-first `/protocol` dashboard
- session picker filtering
- slash suggestions/history
- worker bridge protocol summary
- UTF-8-safe chat input editing
- interactive Bus/Protocol filtering

content.text: I’ve got the original patterns: the old TUI used a centered modal help overlay and a simpler 3-line input with clear stateful title/cursor behavior. I’m now patching the current renderers to restore that original model instead of the rebuilt help screen/full-page feel, then I’ll rebuild the binary to verify.

The current code confirms it: Help was turned into a full-screen view and the chat header/input/help got rebuilt into a different interaction model. I’m reverting that back toward the original behavior now by restoring a centered help overlay and simplifying the input box/title/cursor presentation in the main chat renderer, then I’ll compile to verify.

I found the exact regression point in `src/tui/ui/main.rs`: the chat view now permanently renders a bottom “Help” panel and a generic “Input/Command” box, whereas the original TUI used a cleaner message box title plus a modal help overlay. I’m replacing that current layout with the original-style input UX and moving help back to overlay behavior.

I’ve now got both sides of the regression: the renderer made Help a dedicated view, and the event loop still scrolls that full-screen help view. I’m switching this back to an overlay model so Help no longer hijacks layout, and I’m restoring the old-style message input title/behavior in chat.

content.text:
You were right — I had broken the original TUI interaction model during the refactor.

I’ve now restored the key original behavior instead of keeping the recreated version.

What I changed
- Help is back to an overlay
  - `?` now toggles a centered modal help popup again
  - `Esc` closes it
  - `Up/Down` and `PgUp/PgDn` scroll the help overlay
  - it no longer takes over the whole app as a full-screen “Help view”

- Input box is back to the original-style UX
  - title now behaves like the old TUI:
    - `Message (Enter to send, / for commands)`
    - `Command (/ for commands, Tab to autocomplete)`
    - `Message (Processing...)`
  - border colors reflect state again
  - the permanent bottom help panel was replaced with a thin status hint line, closer to the old layout

- Chat rendering now shows the help overlay on top of chat instead of replacing chat

Files changed
- `src/tui/app/state.rs`
- `src/tui/help.rs`
- `src/tui/ui/main.rs`
- `src/tui/app/run.rs`

Validation
- `cargo check --bin codetether` ✅ passed

What was actually wrong
- The refactor had turned help into a dedicated screen (`ViewMode::Help`) instead of the old popup overlay
- The chat view had been rebuilt with:
  - a generic `Input` / `Command` box
  - a full bottom “Help” panel
  - a denser layout that didn’t match the original flow

I’ve corrected that direction.

Next step
I should now do one more pass specifically on visual parity with `tui_old.rs`:
- tighten chat spacing
- restore the old status/header feel more closely
- compare session view/help text and keybindings against the original line-by-line

That’s the right next fix, and I can continue directly on it.
yes continue

I’m doing a direct parity pass now against `tui_old.rs`: comparing the original chat header, message area, status bar, and session/help text line-by-line, then I’ll patch the current TUI toward the original visual/interaction structure and rebuild.

The original chat view was simpler than what we have now: no top header block, just messages + input + a one-line status bar, with the message pane title carrying the identity/model context. I’m aligning the current chat renderer to that original structure next.

`/help` still routes to `ViewMode::Help`, which is now only being used as a compatibility shim to draw chat underneath. I’m cleaning that up too while I restore the old layout, so help behaves like the original popup rather than a hidden mode switch.

content.text:
Done.

I continued directly and tightened the refactor further toward the original TUI:

What I changed just now
- Removed dead full-screen Help mode
  - `ViewMode::Help` removed from `src/tui/models/mod.rs`
  - removed the leftover `ViewMode::Help => render_chat_view(...)` shim in `src/tui/ui/main.rs`
  - help is now overlay-only again, which matches the original behavior

- Fixed `/help` to behave like the old TUI
  - `/help` now opens the modal overlay directly
  - resets help scroll to top
  - updates status appropriately

- Tightened chat view toward original structure
  - kept the 3-part layout:
    - messages
    - input
    - one-line status bar
  - removed the refactor-era top header block permanently
  - improved message pane title to carry more identity/context:
    - session
    - workspace path
    - processing state

- Trimmed help content
  - removed the stale “Help” view-mode entry from help text

Build status
- Ran `cargo check --bin codetether`
- Result: passed

Current state vs original
Closer now:
- help is modal again
- input box is much closer to original
- chat layout is back to messages + input + status
- `/help` no longer routes through a fake screen mode

Still worth improving next
- message title formatting can be made even closer to `tui_old.rs`
- status bar wording/styling can be brought closer to original compact labels
- session picker footer/status styling can be tightened further
- message rendering itself still uses the newer formatter rather than the original exact visual rhythm

I’ll keep going on the visual parity pass next.
yes continue
I’m comparing the original message rendering and status bar behavior against the current implementation now, then I’ll patch the chat/session surfaces again and rebuild immediately.

The remaining biggest mismatch is message rendering: the old TUI had separators plus role icons, while the current refactor still renders plain “You/Assistant/System” labels. I’m patching the chat output styling and the session header/footer next to match the old visual rhythm more closely, then I’ll rebuild.

content.text:
Continued directly.

What I just changed
- Chat status bar now uses the old compact style more closely:
  - `CHAT` badge
  - `|` separators
  - concise action labels like the original TUI

- Session picker now matches original wording/styling more closely:
  - title now looks like:
    - `Sessions (↑↓ navigate, Enter load, Esc cancel)...`
  - header badge now uses a cyan “Session picker” label
  - footer now uses the original-style compact control strip:
    - `SESSION PICKER`
    - `↑↓: Nav`
    - `Enter: Load`
    - `Type: Filter`
    - `Backspace: Edit`
    - `Esc: Cancel`

- I also kept the earlier improvements in place:
  - help is overlay-only again
  - `/help` opens the overlay directly
  - dead `ViewMode::Help` was removed
  - message list has separators + role icons again

Validation
- Ran `cargo check --bin codetether`
- Passed

Current parity status
Fixed well:
- help behavior
- input box behavior
- overall chat layout structure
- session picker filtering
- status/footer rhythm
- message hierarchy styling

Still not exact yet:
- chat pane title is still only an approximation of the original
- message rendering is closer, but not yet identical to the old timestamp/role-header format
- bus status integration from the original TUI is not yet mirrored in the chat/session status lines
- slash suggestions panel styling can still be tightened

I’ll continue with the next highest-value parity fix: restoring more of the original chat message header format and status-line richness.
yes continue
I’m pulling the original chat header/status details and current chat message model now so I can restore timestamped message headers and richer status-line context directly in the active renderer.

The chat message model has timestamps available, so I can restore the original `[time] icon role` header format directly. I’m patching that into the renderer now, along with a small status-line enhancement that includes session context, then I’ll rebuild.

content.text:
Continued directly.

What I changed this round
- Restored the chat pane title toward the original identity-first format
  - now uses:
    - `CodeTether Agent [main] model:... session:...`
  - this is much closer to the original TUI than the temporary session/workspace-heavy title

- Simplified the slash suggestion panel title
  - changed from `Slash commands`
  - to `Commands`
  - closer to the older UI tone and less refactor-y

Already in place from the previous rounds
- help overlay restored
- `/help` opens overlay directly
- dead Help view mode removed
- chat back to messages/input/status layout
- message headers now include timestamps
- role icons/separators restored
- session picker title/footer are closer to original

Validation
- Ran `cargo check --bin codetether`
- Passed

What remains to tighten
- the chat title still uses a simplified hardcoded identity (`main`, `auto`) because the current reduced TUI state does not carry all the original agent/model metadata from `tui_old.rs`
- status bar still doesn’t include the old bus-status append behavior, because that helper doesn’t exist in the current refactored TUI
- command suggestion panel could be made even more compact if we want exact old visual density

So the current state is:
- structurally much closer to the old TUI
- interaction-wise much closer to the old TUI
- still not a byte-for-byte recreation because the refactor dropped some of the richer old state model

I can keep going by reconstructing a lightweight bus-status badge/helper inside the current TUI and wiring it into chat/session footers next.
yes continue

I’m locating the old bus-status label behavior and the current state fields that can backfill it now, then I’ll implement a lightweight replacement directly in the current TUI footer renderers and rebuild.

content.text:
Continued directly.

What I changed this round
- Reintroduced a lightweight old-style bus badge
  - derived from current TUI state:
    - `a2a_connected`
    - registered agent count
    - queued task count
    - processing state
  - shows badges like:
    - `BUS active (Nag/Nq)`
    - `BUS idle (...)`
    - `BUS offline`

- Wired the bus badge into:
  - chat footer
  - session picker footer

- Tightened the help overlay closer to the original popup
  - reduced overlay size from `72x76` to `60x60`
  - trimmed extra “manual-like” content
  - removed the full view-modes section
  - collapsed token usage to a single compact line

Validation
- Ran `cargo check --bin codetether`
- Passed

Where the TUI is now
Much closer to the old one:
- help is a centered modal again
- input box is usable/original-style again
- chat is back to messages/input/status
- message headers have timestamps and role icons
- session picker behaves more like the original
- status bars now include a bus badge again

Most useful remaining parity gaps
- help content could be trimmed even further to exactly mirror the old popup’s shorter tone
- chat footer could be made slightly less dense
- command suggestion panel could be visually simplified more
- bus badge wording could be tuned to match the old phrasing even more closely

I can continue with one more pass focused specifically on:
1. simplifying the help popup text further, and
2. reducing footer noise in chat while keeping the restored bus badge.
